metadata.title: Why Quicksort's Average Hides Inside a Harmonic Sum
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = harmonic sum / logarithmic bound; CRIMSON = naive n-squared intuition
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no indicator-variable derivation, no worst-case analysis, no in-place sorting detail. Wonder register.

beats.narration_text: med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: Quicksort potentially compares every pair — which sounds quadratic. Yet doubling the list barely more than doubles the work. A harmonic sum is hiding inside.

card.copy: Why Quicksort's Average Hides Inside a Harmonic Sum

beats.narration_text: For a list of n elements, quicksort does at most n times n-minus-1 over 2 comparisons in the worst case — that's O(n²). But on average, with a random pivot, it's far fewer. Why?

production_viz.label: n^2/2 worst case vs actual average comparisons bar comparison; CRIMSON naive, TEAL actual

beats.narration_text: Each pair of elements — i and j in sorted order, with i less than j — is compared only if one of them is the first pivot chosen from the window containing both. The probability of this is 2 divided by j-minus-i-plus-1.

card.copy: Why does doubling barely double the work?
card.sub: Each pair compared with probability 2/(window size)

beats.narration_text: Pairs that are adjacent in sorted order: window size 2, probability 1 — almost always compared. Pairs far apart: large window, small probability. The comparisons are not uniformly distributed.

production_viz.label: Sorted array with pair distance arrows; adjacent pair probability=1 (TEAL); far pair probability=2/n (faint); probability heatmap

beats.narration_text: Sum the comparison probabilities over all pairs. For each element at rank i, sum over all j greater than i: 2/2 plus 2/3 plus dot dot dot plus 2/n — approximately 2 natural-log n. This is a harmonic series truncated at n.

production_viz.label: Harmonic series 2/2 + 2/3 + ... + 2/n displayed as rectangles summing to 2 ln n; TEAL bars

beats.narration_text: The harmonic series H_n equals 1 plus 1/2 plus 1/3 plus dot dot dot plus 1/n, and it is bounded above by the integral of 1/x from 1 to n, which equals natural-log n. The rectangles of height 1/k fit under the curve y equals 1/x.

production_viz.label: Curve y=1/x with rectangles of height 1/k underneath; area = ln n label; TEAL curve, TEAL rectangles

beats.narration_text: Sum over all n starting indices: total comparisons at most n times 2 natural-log n, which equals 2n natural-log n. Since natural-log n equals log base e of n, approximately 1.44 log base 2 of n, this is about 2.88 n log₂ n average comparisons.

production_viz.label: 2n ln n formula derivation; conversion to 2.88 n log2 n; TEAL formula panel

beats.narration_text: For n equals 10: 2 times 10 times natural-log 10 is about 46 comparisons expected. Actual possible pairs: 45. For n equals 1000: expected about 13,800 versus worst case 499,500. The average is 36 times better than worst case at n equals 1000.

production_viz.label: n=10: expected 46 vs 45 pairs. n=1000: 13,800 expected vs 499,500 worst. 36x improvement bar; TEAL=average, CRIMSON=worst

beats.narration_text: The 2n natural-log n result comes entirely from the observation that two elements are compared only if one is the first pivot in their common window. This is a probabilistic argument, not a recurrence. It requires no master theorem.

production_viz.label: Comparison condition: first pivot in window = comparison happens; probability derivation flowchart; TEAL

beats.narration_text: Each pair compared with probability 2 over window size. Harmonic sum per element: approximately 2 natural-log n. Total: 2n natural-log n. The 1/x integral bounds the harmonic series. O(n log n) average, proven by counting.

card.sub: Harmonic sum bounded by the 1/x integral

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
